question_text: A student dissolves sugar in hot water. After the water cools, crystals reappear. Is this reversible or irreversible? Justify your answer.
correct_answer: This is a reversible change because the sugar can be recovered in its original form. When water evaporates, the sugar particles are no longer dissolved and reform as crystals.
explanation: Although a new mixture is formed when sugar dissolves, this is a physical change—no new substances are created. The sugar molecules remain unchanged and can be separated from water by evaporation, proving reversibility. This distinguishes physical changes (reversible) from true chemical changes.
